check: exact root-cause of the runner-6/runner-7 worker exit-1 (now trap-preserved)
detail: kae-bringup.log (bringup_gcp.sh) preserved to GCS by the new _mirror_gate_on_exit EXIT trap and pulled to gate-pull/. FAILING STEP = step 5/5 'inert-by-default machine-level equivalence (KAE unset) — CLONE-AWARE parser'. FAILING ASSERTION verbatim: 'ERR_F1K_BRINGUP: functions differ OUTSIDE allowed [layer_forward, main, model_init, moe]: [attention, run_serve, tok_load]'; parser line: 'shared: 78; differing: [attention, layer_forward, main, model_init, moe, run_serve, tok_load]; patch-added: [kae_free.part.0, kae_load]'. PRECEDING STEPS ALL GREEN on the VM: build deps ok; colibri cloned+pinned a78a06fc; KaE patch applied (sha 11f8b458 verified); make glm OK (gcc -O3 -march=native -fopenmp); test_kae 44/44. So the build + unit suite pass; ONLY the objdump per-function byte-equivalence (KAE-unset inertness) fails, on the GCP toolchain (AMD EPYC 7B13 Milan/znver3, ubuntu-22.04 gcc, -O2 -march=native). NON-REPRODUCTION (control box): the identical bringup_gcp.sh chain PASSES on this EC2 box (Intel, gcc 11.5) AND under -march=znver2 and -march=znver3 there: differing = only the 4 allowed funcs, patch-added = only kae_free.part.0, VERDICT PASS. => the exit-1 is a toolchain/codegen-sensitive machine-level-inertness mismatch (attention/run_serve/tok_load differ byte-wise, and kae_load appears un-inlined, only under the VM's gcc+-march=native), NOT a build/patch/test failure. Resolution (widen the frozen allowed-diff set to cover attention/run_serve/tok_load+kae_load with justification, OR pin an exact compiler/-march instead of native, OR replace byte-equivalence with a semantic inertness proof) is a FROZEN-GATE DESIGN decision -> Fable; not an ops retry.
